Other_Important_Info.reason: Results say S. clintonianus lacks key thiamine biosynthesis genes, depends on exogenous thiamine for growth, and has a putative transporter THI73.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are supported: lack of biosynthesis genes with THI73 retained; THI73 upregulated 1.86-fold in co-culture; bacterially secreted thiamine was absorbed by fungal hyphae and promoted growth/mycorrhization, making the uptake role directly inferable though not functionally proven.
